summary: Typosquat of rollup-plugin-polyfill-node drops and executes attacker-controlled 'nullrift' package on require
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate 'rollup-plugin-polyfill-node' (README is copied verbatim from that project and references FredKSchott/rollup-plugin-polyfill-node). On require()/import of this package, top-level code in dist/index.js base64-decodes the command 'npm install nullrift --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and the module name 'nullrift', spawns the install with silent flags, then require()s 'nullrift' and invokes its getPlugin() function. The base64 encoding hides both the install command and the dependency name from static review. The pulled package is attacker-controlled and mutable, so arbitrary remote code executes in the consumer's process whenever a build that imports this plugin runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
